quote.text: The truth is rarely pure and never simple.
quote.author: Oscar Wilde, The Importance of Being Earnest (1895)

macro.title: What It Means — VWRA, Global Bonds, Gold & BTC

reads.label: VWRA
reads.text: equities shrugged off the hawkish shift on AI-chip optimism — valuations still assume rate-cut conditions the Fed now disputes.

reads.label: Global bonds
reads.text: yields whipsawed on policy repricing, not a steady trend — up on hawkish minutes, down on oil relief and a Tokyo pension-fund assist.

reads.label: Gold
reads.text: holding roughly $4,060–4,160, its Hormuz safe-haven bid offset by dollar strength; forecasters range from $4,900 to $6,000 by year-end.

reads.label: BTC
reads.text: unmoved near $64,340, showing no clear correlation to the rates repricing.

macro.note: Education, not advice; forecasts are forecasts.

stories.headline: Third strike wave hits Iran as Hormuz status splits into rival realities
stories.dek: Washington and Tehran traded a fresh round of attacks over the weekend, with Iranian strikes reaching five Gulf states. The Strait of Hormuz is now simultaneously "closed" and "open," depending on who is asked.

stories.kicker: The Macro Desk · Four Instruments, One Reading
stories.headline: Fed's dot-plot flips: rate-cut odds vanish, September hike bets jump to roughly 70%
stories.dek: Hawkish June FOMC minutes, a Hormuz-rattled oil market and a blockbuster SK Hynix Nasdaq debut reset the board for equities, bonds, gold and bitcoin — ahead of Tuesday's US CPI and bank earnings season.

stories.headline: The ugliest shark on Earth was filmed alive for the first time in 125 million years of trying
stories.dek: A goblin shark swimming free at 4,058ft off Jarvis Island ends a run of accident-only sightings — plus Webb's four-years-in-the-making portrait of a galaxy crash, and a crystal that proves the quantum world doesn't stop at the atom.

stories.headline: Circle Line closes the loop: CCL6 opens, cutting up to 10 minutes off Marina Bay trips
stories.dek: Three new stations — Keppel, Cantonment and Prince Edward Road — opened for passenger service on Sunday, completing a 39km, 33-station ring that finally links HarbourFront directly to Marina Bay.

stories.headline: HDB resale index falls for a second straight quarter — even as million-dollar flats hit a record share
stories.dek: Fresh Q2 data complicates the resale story: the official price index is down 0.3% quarter-on-quarter, but June's flash reading shows a market bifurcating between mature-estate trophy flats and a softer non-mature segment — even as a mega Bayshore GLS site and a new Lentor preview keep private supply flowing.

stories.kicker: Technology · The SK hynix ticker switchover
stories.headline: SKHYV becomes SKHY today — and the memory maker's US investors get their first Monday to decide if $168 was a fair price
stories.dek: SK hynix's Nasdaq-listed shares trade under a new symbol from today, two trading days after a $26.5bn debut. The number Wall Street is now arguing over: how much of the AI-memory boom is already in the price.

stories.kicker: The Kit · A $35 tracker card, finally
stories.headline: Eufy's first Find Hub tracker lands two years late — and undercuts everyone by half
stories.dek: The SmartTrack Card E40 is Anker's Eufy brand joining Android's Find Hub network at $34.99, roughly the price of two cinema tickets and well under what Tile and Samsung charge for the same wallet-card form factor.

stories.kicker: Connected Home · Two hubs, two philosophies
stories.headline: Home Assistant rewrote its Matter server from scratch. Apple Home added 4K and let Apple Intelligence watch your camera clips. Same week, opposite bets
stories.dek: Home Assistant 2026.7 went stable on 1 July with a from-scratch Matter server and plain-language automations. Days later, Apple shipped what reporting calls its biggest Home update in years. One is open-source infrastructure; the other is a walled garden getting more capable — and pricier.

stories.headline: The Best-Laid Cabin: Singapore Airlines' New Business Seat Slips to 2027, Even as the A350 Lands This Month
stories.dek: Plus: a German audio house's biggest launch yet, and the wait goes on for a hammer price on the rarest Daytona to reach an Australian block.

stories.headline: Madonna Beats Her Own Projection: "CONFESSIONS II" Debuts at 134,000 Units for a 10th Billboard No.1
stories.dek: Moana's live-action remake confirms a soft ~$37M opening as Nolan's "The Odyssey" heads into its review embargo, and the first dissenting voice cracks the reaction consensus.

stories.headline: The Screen-Time Study Everyone's Quoting — And What Its Numbers Actually Say
stories.dek: A new analysis of 50,231 American children puts hard figures on heavy screen use and mood. Before rewriting tonight's house rules, here's what the data can and can't tell you.

stories.headline: Swiping Left on the Other Party: Politics Now Outweighs Chemistry on Dating Apps
stories.dek: A new vignette study of young Americans finds the penalty for dating across party lines is bigger than the reward for dating within it.

stories.headline: Why Doing Many Different Workouts Beats Doing More Of One
stories.dek: Thirty years of tracking more than 100,000 adults finds variety — not just volume — predicts a longer life. Plus: an early, small trial on creatine for the perimenopausal brain.

stories.headline: ENHYPEN's Singapore date finally gets its year — 2027, not 2026
stories.dek: A stadium show long filed under "sometime soon" gets a fixed calendar slot this week, a Jakarta leg of the same tour opens sales first, and Singapore's longest-running ticket chase enters its seventh unresolved issue.

stories.headline: What the Next Ship Reports
stories.dek: Twice this week, two capitals described the same stretch of water in opposite, sincere terms. It's an old problem. The old fix was never propaganda — it was patience, and the discipline of saying "not yet" out loud.
